REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE HEARING IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS IS DENIED AS FOLLOWS: 1. THE PARTIES ENTER INTO A STANDARD PROTECTIVE ORDER RELATING TO DISCLOSURE OF THESE DOCUMENTS AND SHALL BE MADE PART OF THE ORDER. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE FORM OF THE ORDER AND PLAINTIFF SHALL PRESENT IT FOR EXECUTION AT THE HEARING. 2. EACH OF THE THIRD PARTIES WHOSE RECORDS WERE SUBPOENAED SHALL PRODUCE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE FILING OF THE ORDERS ALL DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE BEEN SUBPOENAED TO DEFENSE COUNSEL THE DOCUMENTS FOR A FIRST LOOK. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL MAINTAIN THE ORIGINALS OF ALL THESE DOCUMENTS SO THAT ANY FURTHER DISPUTES, IF ANY, REGARDING THESE DOCUMENTS CAN BE RESOLVED WITHOUT INVOLVING THE THIRD PARTIES. 3. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS RELATED TO DLC, DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO DLC EXCEPT THEY MAY REDACT PORTIONS SUBJECT TO A PRIVILEGE LOG AS TO THIRD PARTY PRIVACY INTERESTS. 4. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS RELATED TO THE MCNABBS, DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS EXCEPT THOSE THAT THEY CONSIDERED PRIVILEGED UNDER THE RIGHT OF PRIVACY AND/OR REDACT PRIVILEGED PORTIONS SUBJECT TO A PRIVILEGE LOG. AT A MINIMUM THEY SHALL PRODUCE THE PERIODIC STATEMENTS FROM EACH BANK. 5. ALL OF THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS MAKING SUBSEQUENT REQUESTS TO DEFENDANTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OR REMOVAL OR REDACTIONS BASED ON GOOD CAUSE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. TAUALA; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE HEARING IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS IS DENIED AS FOLLOWS: 1. THE PARTIES ENTER INTO A STANDARD PROTECTIVE ORDER RELATING TO DISCLOSURE OF THESE DOCUMENTS AND SHALL BE MADE PART OF THE ORDER. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE FORM OF THE ORDER AND PLAINTIFF SHALL PRESENT IT FOR EXECUTION AT THE HEARING. 2. EACH OF THE THIRD PARTIES WHOSE RECORDS WERE SUBPOENAED SHALL PRODUCE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE FILING OF THE ORDERS ALL DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE BEEN SUBPOENAED TO DEFENSE COUNSEL THE DOCUMENTS FOR A FIRST LOOK. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL MAINTAIN THE ORIGINALS OF ALL THESE DOCUMENTS SO THAT ANY FURTHER DISPUTES, IF ANY, REGARDING THESE DOCUMENTS CAN BE RESOLVED WITHOUT INVOLVING THE THIRD PARTIES. 3. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS RELATED TO DLC, DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO DLC EXCEPT THEY MAY REDACT PORTIONS SUBJECT TO A PRIVILEGE LOG AS TO THIRD PARTY PRIVACY INTERESTS. 4. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS RELATED TO THE MCNABBS, DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS EXCEPT THOSE THAT THEY CONSIDERED PRIVILEGED UNDER THE RIGHT OF PRIVACY AND/OR REDACT PRIVILEGED PORTIONS SUBJECT TO A PRIVILEGE LOG. AT A MINIMUM THEY SHALL PRODUCE THE PERIODIC STATEMENTS FROM EACH BANK. 5. ALL OF THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS MAKING SUBSEQUENT REQUESTS TO DEFENDANTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OR REMOVAL OR REDACTIONS BASED ON GOOD CAUSE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. TAUALA; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS FOR DEFENDANT DESIGN LINE CONSTRUCTION DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE HEARING IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS IS DENIED AS FOLLOWS: 1. THE PARTIES ENTER INTO A STANDARD PROTECTIVE ORDER RELATING TO DISCLOSURE OF THESE DOCUMENTS AND SHALL BE MADE PART OF THE ORDER. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE FORM OF THE ORDER AND PLAINTIFF SHALL PRESENT IT FOR EXECUTION AT THE HEARING. 2. EACH OF THE THIRD PARTIES WHOSE RECORDS WERE SUBPOENAED SHALL PRODUCE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE FILING OF THE ORDERS ALL DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE BEEN SUBPOENAED TO DEFENSE COUNSEL THE DOCUMENTS FOR A FIRST LOOK. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL MAINTAIN THE ORIGINALS OF ALL THESE DOCUMENTS SO THAT ANY FURTHER DISPUTES, IF ANY, REGARDING THESE DOCUMENTS CAN BE RESOLVED WITHOUT INVOLVING THE THIRD PARTIES. 3. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS RELATED TO DLC, DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO DLC EXCEPT THEY MAY REDACT PORTIONS SUBJECT TO A PRIVILEGE LOG AS TO THIRD PARTY PRIVACY INTERESTS. 4. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS RELATED TO THE MCNABBS, DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS EXCEPT THOSE THAT THEY CONSIDERED PRIVILEGED UNDER THE RIGHT OF PRIVACY AND/OR REDACT PRIVILEGED PORTIONS SUBJECT TO A PRIVILEGE LOG. AT A MINIMUM THEY SHALL PRODUCE THE PERIODIC STATEMENTS FROM EACH BANK. 5. ALL OF THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS MAKING SUBSEQUENT REQUESTS TO DEFENDANTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OR REMOVAL OR REDACTIONS BASED ON GOOD CAUSE. Hearing held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:
Defendant's request to continue hearing is denied.
Defendant's Motion to Quash Subpoenas is DENIED as follows:
1. The parties enter into a standard protective order relating to disclosure of these documents and shall be made part of the order. Parties to meet and confer on the form of the order and Plaintiff shall present it for execution at the hearing.
2. Each of the third parties whose records were subpoenaed shall produce within 15 days of the filing of the orders all documents that have been subpoenaed to defense counsel the documents for a first look. Defense counsel shall maintain the originals of all these documents so that any further disputes, if any, regarding these documents can be resolved without involving the third parties.
3. Within 30 days of defense counsel's receipt of the documents related to DLC, defense counsel shall produce all documents relating to DLC except they may redact portions subject to a privilege log as to third party privacy interests.
4. Within 30 days of defense counsel's receipt of the documents related to the McNabbs, defense counsel shall produce all documents except those that they considered privileged under the right of privacy and/or redact privileged portions subject to a privilege log. At a minimum they shall produce the periodic statements from each bank.
5. All of this is without prejudice to Plaintiffs making subsequent requests to defendants for production of documents or removal or redactions based on good cause.

2. Each of the third parties whose records were subpoenaed shall produce within 15 days of the filing of the orders all documents that have been subpoenaed to defense counsel the documents for a first look. Defense counsel shall maintain the originals of all these documents so that any further disputes, if any, regarding these documents can be resolved without involving the third parties.
3. Within 30 days of defense counsel's receipt of the documents related to DLC, defense counsel shall produce all documents relating to DLC except they may redact portions subject to a privilege log as to third party privacy interests.
4. Within 30 days of defense counsel's receipt of the documents related to the McNabbs, defense counsel shall produce all documents except those that they considered privileged under the right of privacy and/or redact privileged portions subject to a privilege log. At a minimum they shall produce the periodic statements from each bank.
5. All of this is without prejudice to Plaintiffs making subsequent requests to defendants for production of documents or removal or redactions based on good cause.
